stem: A ISP analysis study measures the download speed (Mbps) for a sample of 25 subscribers. The sample mean is 142.2 Mbps with a sample standard deviation of 27.1 Mbps. However, 2 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 23 observations have a mean of 131.0 Mbps and standard deviation of 21.3 Mbps. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 142.2, 95% CI = [131.0, 153.4]. Without outliers: mean = 131.0, 95% CI = [122.1, 139.9]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [122.1, 153.4]. Point estimate: 136.6 Mbps.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [122.1, 153.4].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
